methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from the Calvert Formation using a grid-based sampling method to ensure representative sampling across different stratigraphic beds.

steps.description: A plane table and alidade were used to determine a horizontal plane over the sampling area.
steps.searchable_summary: establish horizontal plane using plane table and alidade

steps.description: Nine stations were marked in a 3-x 3-station grid with a center to center spacing of 9.5 m (31.2 ft) between stations.
steps.searchable_summary: mark stations in a 3x3 grid with specified spacing

steps.description: Five samples were selected at random within an area centered around each station, resulting in a total of 45 samples from the 9 stations.
steps.searchable_summary: select random samples around each station

steps.description: Samples were 1cm thick wafers taken with a plastic coring tube with an inner diameter of 3.5 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples using plastic coring tube

methods.description: Procedure for washing, splitting, and identifying specimens from sediment samples collected from the Calvert Formation.

steps.description: The 1cm wafers of sediment were washed on a 63 p. screen.
steps.searchable_summary: wash sediment wafers on a 63 p. screen

steps.description: Samples containing fewer than 300 specimens were picked completely; some samples containing more than 300 also were picked completely because of misjudgement of the number of specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: pick specimens from samples with fewer than 300 specimens

steps.description: Most samples containing more than 300-400 benthic specimens were split into an aliquot containing that number using a microsplitter.
steps.searchable_summary: split samples into aliquots using microsplitter

steps.description: The specimens were then picked, placed onto faunal slides, and identified.
steps.searchable_summary: identify specimens and place onto faunal slides
